title: Ravishing Korean Provola Pancake
description: A tangy, juicy brunch pancake blending Korean flavors with creamy provola and a fluffy lamination puff.
instructions: In a bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, salt, and baking powder.
Add warm water and beaten egg, stirring until a smooth batter forms.
Fold in shredded provola cheese, gochujang, sesame oil, and black pepper.
Heat a nonstick skillet over medium heat and lightly oil it with a paper towel.
Pour a ladleful of batter onto the skillet and spread it into a circle about 3 inches in diameter.
Cook for 2-3 minutes until the edges lift and bubbles appear on top.
Flip the pancake and cook another 2 minutes until golden brown.
Repeat with remaining batter to make a second pancake.
Warm the pancakes in the oven at 180°C for 5 minutes before serving.
Plate the pancakes and drizzle extra gochujang if desired.
